column1: What was purchased?;['mattress'
column2: 'mattress'];I purchased this mattress mostly based on prior experience. I had purchased a memory foam mattress from Amazon about 4 years ago and it was perfect - and comfortable. I thought I was buying the same item based on price and description. I bought this mattress about 6 months ago when I moved. It's not the same mattress! As others noted it never expanded to the full 10 inches (mine went to about 9 - which is OK) but the lower corners (especially 2) of the lower-firm-foam didn't fill out much - the corners are only about 4 or 5 inches thick. This does not affect sleeping comfort and actually makes it easier to put the fitted sheets on. At $300 vs. $1200+ for local priced twin memory foam I suppose one can't complaint too much - but I would not have accepted such a defect if I didn't have to send it back - and that's not easy from Alaska. After sleeping on the mattress for 6 months it is very comfortable and still no "dips" in my usual sleeping position - but those corners still bother me every time I change the sheets! Considering the price I would still buy my next memory foam mattress from Amazon but would not buy this brand.

column1: how long have they been using a george foreman grill?;['10 years'
column3: '10 years'];I know a lot of people are comparing this to another
column4: higher end product. But I am going to try and compare it to what most people have: a trust George Foreman Grill. My family has been using the Foreman for 10 years and I never thought I would get rid of it. Guess what went in the trash last week?I love the Big Boss grill more than I ever loved my Foreman and here is why:The plates: These easily go into my dish washer
column5: no problem. And they come clean! On the Foreman it was always a struggle to find room and you basically had to scrub them clean before washing them.Folding open: The Foreman was great for steaks and chicken. This is great for a whole lot more! Anything you could make on the Foreman
column6: you can make on the Big Boss. You can also throw out your dedicated griddle: just fold open the Big Boss and pancakes are just a couple of flips away.Cleaning: The Foreman was a mess. You could not get to the nooks and crannies just due to the way it was put together. The Big Boss folds open easily to get at those spots you were scared to look at on the Foreman.So why 4 stars and not 5? No instruction manual
column7: and no waffle plate. The box said it could make waffles...how you can do that with no waffle plate is beyond me.Overall: a great product that I plan to use for several years!

column1: what did the husband want for the wedding?;['a chocolate fountain'
column2: 'chocolate fountain'];My husband really wanted a chocolate fountain at our wedding
column3: it was one of the only things he had input on. I started looking at the rentals and was shocked that they were about $150 - $200 to rent. We were having a smaller wedding with only about 60 guests and I could not imagine spending that much on a chocolate fountain rental
column4: so I started my quest and found the Wilton 3 Tier Chocolate Fountain.We cleaned it before the wedding
column5: but didn't have a chance to try it out so the wedding day was the trial day. We heated the chocolate pieces and mixed in the oil and there it went we had our own chocolate fountain (I paid a couple college kids to keep the chocolate going as well as keep the other dishes on our buffet table full and they just used zip lock bags to melt the chocolate and mix in the oil). We have since used it several other times. My husband is in charge of cleaning it
column6: he says you have to treat it kind of like a paint sprayer when you clean it and make sure that all of the parts get cleaned so that it doesn't clog up. With his method of cleaning we have had no problems and do a lot more chocolate fondue than we did before we had the fountain.I have since experimented with the fondue recipe a bit and use a mix of chocolate pieces
column7: marshmallows and brandy then add a little oil if it is too thick. The trick is to make sure that the chocolate is not too thick and heavy for the pump that takes the chocolate to the top of the fountain.One more note
column8: I have seen some of the fancy fountains at other events and in some buffets and I would say if you are looking to use this for a large (More than 75 people) gathering you probably need to go with something bigger
column9: but for smaller groups this is a hit.

column5: so I started my quest and found the Wilton 3 Tier Chocolate Fountain.We cleaned it before the wedding
column6: but didn't have a chance to try it out so the wedding day was the trial day. column7: he says you have to treat it kind of like a paint sprayer when you clean it and make sure that all of the parts get cleaned so that it doesn't clog up. column8: marshmallows and brandy then add a little oil if it is too thick. The trick is to make sure that the chocolate is not too thick and heavy for the pump that takes the chocolate to the top of the fountain.One more note
column9: I have seen some of the fancy fountains at other events and in some buffets and I would say if you are looking to use this for a large (More than 75 people) gathering you probably need to go with something bigger

column1: What kind of texture do the sheets have?;['very fine suede'
column3: 'suede'];The sheets in my house must be comfortable and easily changed. I hate sheets that require a lot of muscle to get around mattress corners. These sheets while they fit well and snugly have the deep corners that mattresses today require for ease of bed making. What I found interesting was that the sheets almost have the texture of very fine suede like material while being a cotton sheet.This means the color looks richer also. I prefer these to my Egyptian cotton sheets. I have many large dogs and one occasionally puts a paw or two on the bed in the morning and one will sneak into it if given the opportunity. These sheets hold up to the claws unlike the Egyptian cotton yet seem just as soft.They wash up well too and stay soft.

column1: what happened to the kitchen that required putting food into plastic boxes?;['Attack of the Mouse Horde'
column2: 'Attack of the Mouse Horde'];I'm horribly disorganized. Well
column3: not really
column4: but my husband couldn't seem to find things on the wire shelves in our kitchen. Until [dramatic music] Attack of the Mouse Horde.Suddenly it was necessary to put foodstuffs and other items in large plastic boxes; even though they are translucent enough to see through
column5: they're still a little opaque. At a distance it was difficult to discern exactly what was where. I knew where the bread box was
column6: but he'd end up taking two or three off the shelf when he wanted to make a sandwich.Incidentally
column7: the box idea worked. We are no longer bothered by the [fading dramatic music] Mouse Horde.Enter Ziggyboard. I'd gotten these spice jar labels to ID spices in a glass jar set; the idea of being able to rewrite is ideal for my purpose. With the left-over labels (there are plenty of labels if you're only doing spices) I could ID the plastic boxes. Interesting result and totally unexpected.I can find things myself far more easily. Husband can find the bread and now knows where to stash things; in the past I would periodically put things away he'd left out. With such clarity of organization now present in my kitchen
column8: I began looking for other things to use labels on
column9: and whole cabinets were cleared
column10: cleaned
column11: and reorganized.I've now purchased the rectangular label version in an attempt to tame the worst of all problem rooms: my sewing/crochet/art studio. Honestly
column12: one would think I had a hoarding problem or something...

column1: what does the top layer of this mattress cover feel like?;['a terry cloth towel'
column3: 'terry cloth towel'];I've tried so many mattress covers and found they're either noisy or cause my sheets to not fit correctly. I found neither of these a problem with this cover. The top layer is like a terry cloth towel and the sides are a mesh like material. It fit our king size mattress perfectly and my sheets slid on without a problem. I'm planning to purchase more for my children's twin beds. We have cheap covers on those at the moment and their sheets are always sliding up. I haven't tested the water resistant factor
column4: and hopefully won't have to. With that said I think it could handle a minor spill
column5: but honestly anything but a plastic cover isn't going to protect 100%. I for one refuse to sleep on plastic so hopefully this will help prolong the life of my new mattress.Update: Puppy barfed on our bed
column6: and this cover did an excellent job of protecting our treasured mattress.

I still have pieces from all the other sets but a recent Earthquake caused the shelf they were on to crash breaking several of the bowls
column5: so we bought this set which has all the smaller bowls (our largest bowl is intact and gets used daily even though it is over 40 years old.) Anyway Duralex is my first choice for mixing and prep bowls and I intend to get sets for my children when they leave home so they won't take mine.

column1: Are they more likely freezing or canning the contents of the ball jars?;['freezing'
column2: 'freezing'];This is one of those essentials items I use often. I've tried to list all the things I use it for here--a lot more than you'd think. Of course I use it to fill my Ball jars even though I am more likely freezing the contents than canning them. The good thing about this gadget is that it not only keeps the rim clean for sealing but when the contents reach the bottom of the funnel when placed in my wide mouth jars
column3: I know to stop filling so I have room for expansion when freezing something.I also keep another one of these in my birdfeed container because one of my feeders is a narrow tube that used to take very careful filling and still I spilled much seed. With this funnel the filling is fast and easy.In the kitchen
column4: I put lots of things in Ball jars for storage like rice
column5: grits
column6: rolled oats
column7: powdered sugar
column8: baking soda
column9: popcorn
column10: chocolate bits
column11: seeds and nuts. They stay fresh and secure this way. Filling the jars is easy and efficient with this funnel. No spillage. I make my own salad dressing and mayonnaise and put those in the half pint jars
column12: and use both the pint and quart jars for other things. I may squeeze a dozen grapefruits using the attachment on my food processor or use a juicer attachment for grape juice. The food processor bowl doesn't have a pour spout so transferring the juice to a jar requires help from this funnel. Even pouring sugar into a measuring cup can be messy business if a lump gets in the way
column13: so I put the funnel in the mouth of the cup first.One thing the funnel is absolutely essential for is when I buy things in bulk at the health food store. Getting whatever from those flimsy plastic bags into a jar is a real challenge.I keep one of these funnels in the garage for filling the gas tank on my tractor and my leaf vacuum. Holding those big heavy containers just right to get the nozzle in the tank often alludes me. The funnel helps me get started without spillage. Another thing I use the funnel for is getting the dirt in small pots for starting seeds early in the spring. I do that inside and really don't want the soil spilled around.All in all I've found so many uses for this funnel
column14: I've ended up with several. It's a very useful tool.

column1: What size is the pan?;['8"'
column2: '8"'];I own this pan in the 8" and just love it. I was looking for a good quality egg pan and this is it! I use it everyday. All the teflon on my other pans eventually peeled up and I was afraid we were eating the product! I tried other types of pan
column3: but my eggs always stuck. I went down to the outlet and found this pan. Yes
column4: it was pricey but if I never have to buy another egg pan...I'm happy.The very first time I used it for my eggs
column5: they did seem to stick a bit and I started to get discouraged...don't. The pan will season itself with each cooking. By just the third time of egg cooking... no sticking what so ever! The pan has a nice beefy weight feel to it without being heavy and looks beautiful. I could not be without this pan anymore. Le Creuset continues to make high quality products and you will not be disappointed with their non stick pans.Update: I did purchase the 12" too. Needed more room for cooking eggs for more than one person. Just as perfect as the 8" no sticking what so ever even with the first use on this pan. Definitely my favorite pans. You will not be disappointed if your looking for a superior non stick.

column1: What was wrong with the pan when he first got it?;['did not come properly "Pre-Seasoned."'
column3: 'did not come properly "Pre-Seasoned."'];I wish I could give this pan a better review however
column4: the one I received did not come properly "Pre-Seasoned." I can detect rust underneath which was not cleaned off prior to factory seasoning. Some of the rust is alredy surfacing. I will have to remove the old seasoning and rust and then properly season before actually using it. I have noticed that Lodge is not pre-seasoning their products as well as they used to. The last three items I purchased via AMAZON have the same problem. AMAZON should contact Lodge regarding this continuing trend. I suggest anyone interested in purchasing any of the Lodge line of products to consider the aforementioned first. One reason Lodge pre-seasoned products are higher is becasue they are supposed to be pre-seasoned and ready for immediate use.

column1: How has the author felt previously about similar products?;['disappointed'
column3: 'disappointed'];I must admit I had my doubts at first. I have used a lot of similar products in the past and have always been disappointed. This is the first wine chiller that really works! As cold as the wine is it will remain at the same constant temperature during an entire meal. So far it has passed the one hour test. The only minor difficulty in in removing the bottle from the cooling container. the chilling panel hugs the bottle so tighly it is a little difficult removing it. But it is probably because of this design that it is so effective in keeping the bottle of wine so cold. It is ideal for white wines.

column3: 'providing several feet of tubing to feed into a bulk bottle of hand soap'];While I can't speak to the long-term reliability of this product (only had it a little while)
column4: I can say that providing several feet of tubing to feed into a bulk bottle of hand soap was a stroke of genius. All of these in-sink soap dispensers have inexplicably narrow-necked bottles which makes them very difficult to fill without crawling underneath the sink and unscrewing the bottle. If you try to fill from above with typically-thick consumer hand soap

column6: it'll take you forever. Prime from a glass of water
column7: and when it starts dispensing
column8: switch over to the soap.

column1: What was the author's initial complaint?;['there were no instructions'
column3: 'no instructions'];The package that this arrived in was much bigger than I expected
column4: but it was well protected. My initial complaint would be that there were no instructions. Not even a "how to care for your new pan" pamphlet floated out of the packing material. I would think that for an item at this price point you would at least tell the purchaser how to care for it.The pan is solid. I liked the stainless steel handle for the "ooh shiny" and a bit of high tech effect. I washed it before I used it. I put it on the double burner on my ceramic top. column3: 'SCVP609-KLS Smart-Pot 6-Quart Oval-Shaped Slow Cooker with Little Dipper'];I am quite mystified at the terrible reviews this crock pot has recieved. I bought the largerCrockpot SCVP609-KLS Smart-Pot 6-Quart Oval-Shaped Slow Cooker with Little Dipper
column4: Whitefor my mom about two years ago and we have used it numerous times to cook many things and have never once had any issues with it. I bought this smaller round one at a local department store and it performs just as well as the larger one.The only reason food will boil over is if you overfill it or you use too much water. Meats and vegetables produce their own moisture when cooking inside a crock so adjust the water you add accordingly. For instance
column5: when I make a beef roast I only add about a third cup of water. Naturally the crock is going to get hot because it has to cook the food over an extended period of time
column6: especially if you set it for 4 or 6 hours on the HIGH setting.Keep in mind that not all reviews are accurate. The reason for this is that quite often people will simply not read the directions or display any common sense when using the product. I do not mean to make others sound bad
column7: but this has been my experience when purchasing products online (or anywhere for that matter).

column1: What is the price of the repair to the mixer?;['$129'
column3: '$129'];I have just paid $129 to have Kitchenaid repair my two-year old 600 series mixer. It died while making cookie dough. This is my second 600-series mixer; Kitchenaid (to their credit) swapped out the first under warranty after it started behaving erratically.The primary use of this mixer is to make 4 loaves of bread/week. I never expected miracles: bread flour is hard to knead and I'm careful about kneading times and quantities. This is not a professional machine
column4: it's a home appliance. Nevertheless
column5: it died after only a moderate amount of careful use.So
column6: I have reluctantly come to agree with the Kitchenaid bashers here. Based on my experience
column7: the current crop of mixers is not high quality and my next mixer will not be Kitchenaid
column8: sorry to say.It's sad to watch an iconic brand destroy its reputation.

column1: How long have they owned the product?;['about three months'
column2: 'about three months'];I was excited to have one machine to make espresso and coffee in a compact size at a reasonable price. Be warned - you get what you pay for. We have had the machine about three months now and we are starting to have some issues with it. The espresso carafe is cracked and it was never dropped or mishandled. The cost to replace it is outrageous. In general
column3: it takes 20 minutes to brew a full pot of coffee and the temperature is never consistent. The coffee pot spills everywhere when you pour due to the venting on the lid. It is pretty easy to clean. But the whole design is odd/poor overall. I am not very pleased with it as a whole. I will not buy another one of these. I will buck up and buy a more expensive one with better features.Just an update. The coffee maker suddenly stopped working completely. We cleaned it repeatedly on a regular basis and have no idea what the problem is. The espresso make still works. I pulled out the old Cuisinart and it works like a charm
column4: even at five years old. You really do get what you pay for. After we get back from vacation
column5: I will be looking for a higher quality dual machine that will hopefully last more than three months. Buyer beware.

column3:
, 'it cleaned a lot of dirt out of the grout'];This is a great product! I use a Swiffer on my hard floors (tile and wood) once a week. However, the first time I used this, a day after using the Swiffer, the dirty water in the collection tank was black! The FloorMate made my hardwood floors look nice and shiny - better than I've seen them in a long time. On my tile, it cleaned a lot of dirt out of the grout; it didn't get it all, but it certainly made it easier to clean the little bit that was left. The unit does a nice job of sucking up all of the water/cleaning fluid from the floor; on the wood, it was completely dry after using, and on the more porous tile I have in my kitchen, it got most of it. Using the machine is simple - very similar to vacuuming, but you do have to go kind of slow to get all of the dirt. I've never used the previous generation of this machine, so I have no idea if this is a downgrade or not, but I have been impressed with it so far, and my floors are much cleaner than they have been since I've owned this house!

column1: What would be a safety concern than can be configured differently in the mixer settings?;["if the mixer automatically reset itself to OFF when it's unplugged."
column3: 'automatically reset itself to OFF'];Purchased this mixer to replace a 10+ year old Kitchen Aid mixer. This mixer does a very nice job & overall I'm glad I purchased it. I gave it only 4 stars because of a potential safety issue - the second time I used this mixer I forgot to plug it in - I'd pushed the ON button & the speed button before I realized that it wasn't plugged in & when I plugged it in the mixer immediately began to run (spattering cake batter everywhere!). I'm thankful that there weren't any little grandchildren's fingers reaching in the bowel to steal a taste of the batter when the mixer automatically started up! It seems to me that it would be much safer if the mixer automatically reset itself to OFF when it's unplugged.

column1: What does the reviewer's brother love making in the microwave?;["s'mores"
column2: "s'mores"];My brother loves making s'mores in the microwave
column3: so I bought him this as a Christmas present. Even though it's not something that's 100% necessary
column4: it was a funny little gift that is actually pretty practical. If you've ever made s'mores in the microwave before
column5: you know that the marshmallow balloons out and you need to keep an eye on it as it "cooks." This little device stops that completely- you add water to the back part
column6: which helps the marshmallow cook evenly
column7: and the "hands" gently compress the s'more as it heats.Personally
column8: after having s'mores made both with and without this little device
column9: I think the marshmallows actually taste better after using the device since they are more evely heated. The chocolate tastes better too since it has more time to melt. Of course
column10: you can save $7 and still make s'mores without it
column11: but if you know someone who loves s'mores
column12: it's a cute and inexpensive gift idea. Highly recommended!

column1: How much counter space does these people have in their living space?;['limited counter space'
column3: 'limited'];we live in an apartment with limited counter space and we've recently started eating healthier. We've been including a lot of fresh fruits and vegetables in our diet. This basket works perfectly to keep our fruit organized and keep a lot of the counter space clean. The spacing of the basket is small enough that it doesn't bruise our fruits and the banana hanger is perfect. The heftiness of the basket will support a good sized bunch even if there aren't loads of fruit in the basket. We tend to keep our tomatoes out on the counter so they always go on top since the fridge ruins the flavor. The item came in no time flat and it's been a wonderful addition to our tiny kitchen

column1: How can you use the grill pan indoors?;['high heat burner to preheat the pan and then grill'
column3: 'use my high heat burner to preheat the pan and then grill'];For approximately three years I have used this grill pan to grill indoors. In fact I used it once again to prepare our Fourth of July barbecue inside the house as the wind was blowing. The wind blows here a lot
column4: so I've used this pan many times to get the barbecue flavor indoors.I use my high heat burner to preheat the pan and then grill steaks
column5: hamburgers or hot dogs on it. The pan leaves authentic grill marks on the food. It looks and pretty well tastes like food grilled on the outdoor barbecue grill.The pan is very easy to clean. I let the pan cool and then add a small amount of water to it. I then simmer the water to help release some of the barbecue mess. I gently scrub the pan with my nylon scrubber and I am done.Great tasting food with a pan that's easy to use and clean.

column1: What product was ordered?;['slow cooker'
column2: 'slow cooker'];WELCOME TO MY COOKING LABOATMEAL: I ordered this slow cooker specifically for making my Irish steel cut oatmeal
column3: for which it is perfect. It cooks the oats in three to four hours on low. If I have pre-ground the oats in my mini processor first
column4: they cook more quickly than otherwise. To pre-grind all one needs do is pulse the oats two or three times in your mini food processor. Right now
column5: I use 2/3 of a cup of oats with a dash of salt to slightly less than 3 cups of water. I taste for doneness at around 3 hours of cooking on low. I serve my cooked oatmeal with Vermont maple syrup
column6: warmed
column7: reduced-fat milk and topped with a few thawed berries or fresh fruit.OATMEAL: The oatmeal that I use is in a newly-opened can of McCann's Irish steel cut oatmeal. I store an opened can in a tightly sealed container in the fridge
column8: and use it up pretty quickly.CROCKERY INSERT: The white crockery insert has handles extending beyond the two sides of the top of the cooker. The handles make it easy to grasp the hot crockery and remove it from the cooker
column9: but you must use oven mitts before grasping the hot handles of the crockery to avoid burning your fingers. Because the bottom of the crockery is rough and could scratch the counter top
column10: I keep a good-sized trivet next to the cooker when I am ready to remove the crockery.COVER: The cover has a tightly sealed black gasket around the bottom which prevents the water from seeping out of the slow cooker. When you lift the cover to test for doneness
column11: the many water droplets that have accumulated at the underneath of the cover are very hot
column12: so avoid burning yourself or your counter tops.

column1: What is this product similar to?;['a regular cooktop'
column3: 'a regular cooktop'];This is a cool little cooking option if you have enough space in your kitchen to hook it up. It seems perfect to me for someone who wants to find out what it's like to cook with an induction stovetop without having to redo their whole kitchen. Our kitchen is a little small so there's not too much space to use it (but that's the fault of our kitchen not the product).I didn't find it to be any different (cooking-wise
column4: speed-wise) than using a regular cooktop but it has the added bonus of being safer and using less energy. The touchscreen is really easy to understand and use and makes using this thing easier.The skillet that came with it is also great (but I'm not cookware snob so I probably wouldn't know "good" from "great" or just "okay").I really love using this product and have used it several times just for the fun of it
column5: but you do kind of have to plan ahead to clear out some space for it if you want to use it. Also seems like it would be convenient for camping (if you had an electrical outlet).

column1: What is the product?;['paper towel holder'
column2: 'paper towel holder'];This paper towel holder is perfect and works exactly how you've always wanted your paper towel holders to work. The spring loaded arm provides just enough tension to allow you to rip off a sheet with one hand while not providing so much force that it is tough for the roll to rotate. The weighted bottom also helps -- no more tipping over the paper towel holder while trying to rip off a sheet. The height of the holder is also shorter than other holders I've used
column3: allowing it to easily fit underneath my upper cabinets.

column1: What is the name of the product manufacturer?;['iRobot corp'
column3: 'iRobot corp'];You need to know that iRobot corp. makes it difficult to change this battery. After removing the screws
column4: I found that the yellow battery case lid is glued to the bottom of the case. You need a couple of straight
column5: small screwdrivers to slowly tap open the glue seam much like a diamond cutter uses a tiny chisel to tap cut a diamond. I had to tap the seam every 1/4 inch to break it loose. Hit it too hard and your cost savings are gone if you break the case at the attachment points - you would need to buy the whole battery pack - so only do this if you are careful and patient. Removing the old battery pack also is challenging if you are not the careful type as it is glued to the base of the case internally. Cutting the wires and soldering the leads is easy but there is no room for any other attachment method so you had better be confident in your soldering skills. This is not a job for the impatient person and you had better have all the right tools but since I am a nerd geek
column6: I enjoyed the challenge and IT WORKED GREAT!! CHOOSE YOUR BATTLES AND KNOW YOUR SKILLS and if this sounds fun and challenging (like I do)
column7: it is a great way to save $40.00 AND set it up to do future battery replacements more easily.

column1: How long has the item been used?;['three months'
column3: 'three months'];I've been using my Breville three months now and love it. I live alone so this is a perfect little oven for me! The only problem I had was my smoke detector kept going off when I used "broil" I learned to overcome this problem by using the lower rack instead of the suggested top rack. (you can always make adjustments with this oven)Pizza is great as I can set the oven for two small slices. What an improvement over using a microwave which totally deletes the taste of pizza.I also bake my own granola in my oven and much more.Kay

column3: it actually takes the place of the whole coffee unit'
column4: 'takes the place of the whole coffee unit'];Over the years I've tried a number of the reusable K-cup gizmos and had come to the conclusion that none of them were designed right. None of the companies making these things seem to take in account that the water actually needs to be in contact with the coffee for some period of time to make a decent cup of coffee. If you take a look at a used k-cup
column5: you'll see that there is a small hole in the top where the water goes in and a small hole in the bottom where the coffee comes out. Most of these filter units have large screened opening that don't allow any pressure to build up like a real k-cup does. So you end up with a very weak cup of coffee even if you over pack the filter.But the design of this Keuring My K-Cup filter is different
column6: it actually takes the place of the whole coffee unit. You can actually get a drinkable cup of coffee from it
column7: even without using a ton of grounds. The one thing I still don't like is having to clean out the basket after every cup. column1: How does this vacuum get power;['charge'];I have purchased just about every cordless vacuum out there including a pricey ($99) cordless that never came close to the manufacturer's claims. After numerous disappointments I finally found the (almost) perfect cordless vacuum.I have seven cats and a combination of hardwood and carpeting in my home and this is the first cordless that I have found to pick up both cat hair on the carpet and on bare floors. It is surprisingly powerful for a cordless and once fully charged seems to last quite a long time until it needs recharging.I only have a few minor complaints being that it is a bit heavier and noisier than other cordless vacuums and the head is somewhat floppy. Considering how well it picks up and how long the charge lasts
column2: the good far outweigh the bad.If you have animals and don't want to be hauling out the big vacuum everyday
column3: this is an excellent choice to consider.

column2: the trick is to heat the oiled pan over a very low flame for a few minutes before frying anything. The food should preferably be at room temperature before its put into the pre-heated pan. For example
column3: if you are frying eggs
column4: don't just remove the eggs from the fridge and crack them into the pan - it's best to first put the whole eggs into a pot of warm water and let them stand to bring them up to temperature.After the pan cools
column5: I wash it with dish washing detergent.

I have it sitting next to my kitchen sink. when the kids want hot water they just flip it on and within a very short time the water is hot. I just refill so it is ready for the next time. I also use it for instant oatmeal and any other water based foods. I love it because it is quick and can add hot water while cooking in no time. No more microwave either. I would definitely recommend to anyone that wants to get rid of the toxns from using a microwave

column1: What is this a good fit for;['a small apartment'
column2: 'I am not in the market for those really expensive Espresso makers'];I carefully read all other reviews on Amazon and everywhere else before purchase. I have had Krups products before and been happier with them than with most of the other brands. I am not in the market for those really expensive Espresso makers but do want my espresso daily. My husband wants his coffee and we are furnishing a small apartment. This looked like the perfect answer. I have only had it one month but so far I am very happy. It makes both good espresso and good coffee and the milk steamer works very well. I saw that many people complained about it but I think they did not read the directions well and practice using it first. It appears that the original steamer design was not good and now Krups has modified and replaced it with a design which does work well.I like cappuccino and I use it by steaming my milk in a large cup first and then making the espresso in another smaller cup and adding it to the cup of milk. This way the cup I drink from is pre-heated and my drink stays hot. If just making espresso then use a small cup and it makes a very nice crema. Enjoy.

column1: What did you purchase this pan with?;['a bread maker'
column3: 'bread maker'];I purchased this in conjunction with a bread maker. I am much happier with this than the bread machine. As long as I make the dough correctly this pan does the job! I have made some french baguettes with this that are better than the ones they sell at Whole Foods and Hannafords(a local supermarket chain). Make sure that you place the dough in the pan for its last rise so you don't deflate it moving it over before cooking. Also
column4: make sure to slice it down the middle or in strips across the top
column5: the top strips work better for me
column6: this is to let it expand properly when cooking. I recommend this pan!

column1: What type of beaters does this vacuum have?;['rotary beaters'
column3: 'rotary'];I bought this vacuum on the recommendation of a major consumer product review organization; while two vacuums had higher ratings
column4: this one cost about half as much. For the most part
column5: I am glad I did not spend the extra money.First and foremost
column6: it has adequate power for the job and the rotary beaters are efficient. I had used an old Hoover for over 12 years and thought I was getting my carpet clean
column7: I was deluded. The difference in the looks of the carpet was immediately apparent. The Eureka was far more effective. Secondly
column8: it is clean. It uses a traditional replaceable bag to collect dust but has a HEPA filter over the exhaust port to collect fine particulate. There was no fine dust in the air or dust smell. The accessories mount on the body of the vacuum
column9: but while convenient they are not completely secured. The brush falls off occasionally. It is very easy to shift from floor cleaning to the hose attachments; you simply turn the handle of a valve on the upper body of the vacuum. The extensions are long enough to reach an 11 ft ceiling. Shifting to different carpet pile heights is also easy and convenient.The consumer organization gave two reasons for not giving this vacuum a top rating. First they said it was not the as easy to maneuver as it might be. This is true; other vacuums are easier to shove about and this one is not power assisted. They also dinged it for being noisier than the two more expensive vacuums. While this is probably true
column10: after the ol' Hoover I was delighted at how quite it was. Also making it quieter would affect the price. The plastic of the body could be a bit more rugged but again this would increase the price. My home is small; if you really spend a lot of time behind a vacuum
column11: you probably want to spend a bit more. This lack of rugged construction probably explains the huge variability in the product reviews. Caveat emptor - the instructions advise calling Eureka customer service and NOT taking the machine back to the place where you purchased it if it is defective. This could be a problem. And - some assembly required.All-in-all
column12: good vacuum
column13: value for money.

column5: I am glad I did not spend the extra money.First and foremost
column6: it has adequate power for the job and the rotary beaters are efficient. I had used an old Hoover for over 12 years and thought I was getting my carpet clean
column7: I was deluded. The difference in the looks of the carpet was immediately apparent. The Eureka was far more effective. Secondly
column8: it is clean. It uses a traditional replaceable bag to collect dust but has a HEPA filter over the exhaust port to collect fine particulate. There was no fine dust in the air or dust smell. The accessories mount on the body of the vacuum
column9: but while convenient they are not completely secured. The brush falls off occasionally. It is very easy to shift from floor cleaning to the hose attachments; you simply turn the handle of a valve on the upper body of the vacuum. The extensions are long enough to reach an 11 ft ceiling. Shifting to different carpet pile heights is also easy and convenient.The consumer organization gave two reasons for not giving this vacuum a top rating. First they said it was not the as easy to maneuver as it might be. This is true; other vacuums are easier to shove about and this one is not power assisted. They also dinged it for being noisier than the two more expensive vacuums. While this is probably true

column1: How big is the digital pocket scale?;['less than 5 inches X 4 inches X less than 1/2 inch high'
column2: 'less than 5 inches X 4 inches X less than 1/2 inch high'
column3: 'less than 5 inches X 4 inches X less than 1/2 inch high'];The American Weigh SC-2KG Digital Pocket Scale is one of the best purchases I ever made. It's even better than I expected. It's small enough to slip into my kitchen drawer when I'm not using it. It's less than 5 inches X 4 inches X less than 1/2 inch high. It has a 4 inch square weighing platform. It's beautifully finished stainless steel with 4 different weight modes
column4: and a particularly easy to read blue lit display. It's so easy to use and store. I tested the accuracy with a 50 g calibrating weight - this scale is absolutely accurate to fractions of whole numbers. I would recommend this to family and friends
column5: and the Amazaon price was marvelous.

column1: Are these nightstands easy to put together?;['I did not expect to take so much time putting them together!!! Seriously it took two people to do it'
column2: 'I did not expect to take so much time putting them together'];Seriously I shopped around for night stands that would look great in our room and wouldn't break my pockets. I bought two of these stands and received them yesterday. I did not expect to take so much time putting them together!!! Seriously it took two people to do it. When putting this stand together do NOT tighten the screws until all the outside pieces are on because if you do the last piece (front one) will not fit. It took forever to try and figure out the issue. While you are screwing in the screws someone else will have to hold the rest of the stand so it doesn't keep falling apart (because the screws weren't tight). This product looks good when its done
column3: but it should have been easier to put together. OH not to mention one of the stands are wobbly; like what is going on! I shop on Amazon faithfully and I am very disappointed.
